mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) maintains a positive trend, sustaining corporate earnings growth and driving equity prices higher over a 60-trading-day horizon, irrespective of short-term fluctuations in other series.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows falls below 0.03, or quarterly GDP growth turns negative for two consecutive quarters without corresponding negative S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labour market deterioration that depresses consumption and investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In rolling instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative or the hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate combined with moderate inflation compresses equity valuations through elevated discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day horizon, especially during a tightening cycle.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) against 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (t-stat > -1.96) over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, decelerating industrial production and slowing employment growth indicate that economic momentum is fading, which reduces forward earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth < 2% and PAYEMS MoM growth < 0.1%
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met in late-cycle regimes, the average 60-day forward return is not negative or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, which directly pressures corporate revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In rolling instances where RSAFS month-over-month is negative, the forward 20-day S&P 500 return exceeds the unconditional median return or is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,500 thousand units historically precedes economic slowdowns; this signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as residential investment contracts.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1500, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts reflect broad economic weakness that sharply depresses equity market valuations over a 20-trading-day horizon, acting as a strong recession signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO QoQ growth negative and HOUST MoM growth negative
failure_condition: In joint decline events, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not more negative than during isolated declines of either series, or the signal has Rank IC below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When employment growth remains positive and the unemployment rate falls concurrently, labour market strength supports rising consumer spending and equity returns over a 20-trading-day horizon during expansions.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0 and UNRATE month-over-month decline
failure_condition: When both conditions hold during expansion regimes, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of positive returns is below 60%.
